name: Acid Neon Terminal-Poster Conference Landing
description: A conference landing page composed as a single continuous poster — the date fills 100vh as a stacked typographic block (one element per line at 30vw, ultra-black weight) with corner-anchored metadata in monospace at each viewport corner (location top-right, URL bottom-left, ticket CTA bottom-right). Below, a full-width black-background marquee scrolls keywords at 140px continuously. The speaker lineup is a black-on-light terminal dump: monospace fixed-column table (# / NAME / ROLE / TALK-SLUG) with neon green (#39FF14) full-row highlight on hover, prefixed by a terminal command line ($ gi summit --list-speakers). Schedule is a compact ledger format (TIME / TITLE / SPEAKER in ruled rows), not cards. Stats are a single inline horizontal strip of value-label pairs, not a grid. Sections divided by 4px solid black rules. No imagery, no icons, no cards, no rounded corners — density is the design. Every pixel is information.

vibes.mood: Feels like the wayfinding signage at a design festival got concatenated with a database query result and projected at 400% — the date is a poster you can read from across the room, the speaker list is stdout from a CLI, the green highlight rows pulse like a rave wristband, and the thick black rules slam sections together like train cars coupling.

meta.audience: Creative technologists who screenshot conference websites and post them in design Discords — people who buy tickets because the page itself was the most interesting design work they saw that week
